title: ecb / monetary policy / inflation outlook | Monetary Policy Report | Economic Bulletin Issue 2, 2026
summary: At its March 19, 2026 meeting, the ECB Governing Council kept its three key ECB interest rates unchanged, maintaining its commitment to ensure inflation stabilises at the 2% target in the medium term. Staff macroeconomic projections show headline inflation revised up to 2.6% in 2026 (from December 2025 projections), while GDP growth was revised down to 0.9% in 2026, reflecting the disruptive impact of the Middle East war on commodity markets, real incomes, and confidence.
